# Venkatesh Kolluru, Ph.D.

> Applied scientist at NASA MSFC ODSI / University of Alabama in Huntsville,
> working across Earth observation, landscape ecology, and hydrology.
> Operationalizes geospatial foundation models (Prithvi-EO-2.0) for satellite
> Earth observation. Open to collaborations across Earth observation,
> geospatial AI, ecosystems, and water.

## Contact
- Email: venkateshkolluru95@gmail.com
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=7Tji1bMAAAAJ&hl=en
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2110-5560
- GitHub: https://github.com/venkateshkolluru95
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/venkatesh-kolluru-855597175/

## Current role
Research Scientist III, NASA MSFC ODSI and University of Alabama in Huntsville
(Jan 2025 – present). Fine-tuned and deployed the 600M-parameter Prithvi-EO-2.0
geospatial foundation model for flood mapping, burn-scar detection, landslide
detection, and multi-temporal crop classification, served live through the PRISM
portal (https://explore.fm.odsi.io/). Reduced per-event inference latency 8.6x
(288s to 30s). Architected a five-stage agentic scientific workflow pipeline
(CARE / CLSW). Authored a red-teaming framework that improved end-to-end model
precision from 4.9% to 98.6%. Benchmarked across 19 globally distributed
out-of-distribution test cases.

## Skills
Python, PyTorch, TerraTorch, Vision Transformers, foundation-model fine-tuning,
LoRA, transfer learning, out-of-distribution evaluation, red-teaming, semantic
segmentation, change detection, agentic AI (OpenAI Custom GPTs, FastMCP, Pydantic
AI), NASA Earthdata APIs (CMR, STAC), GDAL, rasterio, geopandas, Google Earth
Engine, ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, R, MATLAB, SQL, CUDA/V100, statistical attribution
(Granger Causality, Shapley, SEM, geodetector).

## Education
- Ph.D., Environment and Sustainability, University of South Dakota (2021–2024). CGPA 4.0/4.0. Distinguished Dissertation Award.
- M.Tech (Research), Remote Sensing and GIS, NIT Karnataka (2017–2019). Best M.Tech Thesis Award (ISTE).
- B.Tech, Civil Engineering, GMR Institute of Technology (2012–2016).

## Record
27 peer-reviewed publications, 4 under review, 2 book chapters, 34 conference
presentations, 120+ peer reviews. Awards include the 2025 AGU Open Science
Recognition Prize and the USD Distinguished Dissertation Award.
